_meta.purpose: Baseline allowlist for JA-94 (per-example structural-marker presence). RESOLVED 2026-05-17 (Phase A) — all 14 prior BUG-006-CANDIDATE entries addressed via example replacement. JA-94 enforces marker-presence unconditionally; this file's baseline_failing_examples array is empty and serves as a RESOLVED snapshot.

classification_legend.BUG-006-CANDIDATE: The example's `ja` doesn't contain any structural marker for the parent pattern. Most likely the example was incorrectly filed under this pattern; the right fix is to either (a) replace the example with one that demonstrates the parent pattern, or (b) move the example to its correct pattern (if it demonstrates a different pattern's structure).

_meta.next_review_trigger: Any grammar.json edit that adds new examples. JA-94 will trip if a new example doesn't contain ≥1 marker. Either (a) the new example is a legitimate demonstration via a non-canonical form → expand the pattern's markers in data/pattern_markers.json, or (b) the new example is BUG-006-class wrong → fix the example.

_audit_summary.follow_on_target: RESOLVED 2026-05-17 (Phase A): all 14 BUG-006-CANDIDATE examples replaced with parent-pattern-demonstrating ones. JA-94 now enforces marker-presence unconditionally on the current corpus.
